title: cerastium ibukiense
intro: Cerastium ibukiense is a rare alpine plant species native to Japan, belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Japan, for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cerastium ibukiense are said to include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. While there is limited information available, herbal preparations of Cerastium ibukiense are typically made by infusing the plant's leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes.
